trends.name: Gen Z as Technology's New Economic Center
trends.description: Gen Z (86% identify tech as essential to their lives) becoming the dominant consumer force shaping technology product design, marketing, and investment.

trends.name: Global Connectivity Acceleration
trends.description: 5.4 billion people projected online by 2027, with the bulk of new users coming from emerging markets — reshaping digital product design and creating new economic power centers.

signals.name: AI and Robotics Co-Evolution at CES
signals.description: CES 2024 revealing AI and robotics increasingly presented as integrated systems rather than separate domains — a convergence signaling coming operational fusion.

signals.name: Gen Z Splurging on Tech Over Other Categories
signals.description: Gen Z demonstrating higher propensity to prioritize technology spending versus other discretionary categories, validating tech as the dominant consumer value category for the cohort.

trends.name: Crypto Asset Class Institutionalization
trends.description: Cryptocurrency transitioning from alternative to institutional mainstream following ETF approval, tokenization growth, and stablecoin payment integration at scale.

trends.name: Stablecoin Integration into Global Payments
trends.description: Stablecoins achieving meaningful integration into global payments infrastructure, moving beyond crypto-native use toward cross-border settlement and remittance at scale.

signals.name: Spot Bitcoin ETF Approved in US 2024
signals.description: US regulatory approval of spot Bitcoin ETFs marking a watershed moment for institutional access and signaling broader regulatory normalization of crypto assets.

signals.name: Tokenization of Financial Products Accelerating
signals.description: Real-world asset tokenization — bonds, funds, real estate — expanding rapidly as blockchain infrastructure matures and regulatory clarity improves.

signals.name: Pro-Crypto US Political Environment Post-2024 Election
signals.description: 2024 US election result creating favorable regulatory environment for crypto industry, potentially enabling significant policy and infrastructure advances in 2025.

trends.name: Layer 2 Blockchain Scaling
trends.description: Layer 2 networks maturing to handle the throughput required for mainstream adoption, making blockchain infrastructure viable for high-volume financial and commercial applications.

signals.name: Tokenization of Real-World Assets Beginning
signals.description: Traditional financial assets beginning to be represented on blockchain infrastructure, bridging crypto-native systems with conventional capital markets.

entries.filename: COLIN LEWIS_The Seven Challenges for Retail Media in 2024.pdf

trends.name: Retail Media as Primary Ad Infrastructure
trends.description: Retail media networks transitioning from niche opportunity to the dominant advertising infrastructure, rivaling search and social in scale and strategic importance.

signals.name: Retail Media Coverage Reaching Financial Press
signals.description: Retail media moving from industry journals to Financial Times and WSJ coverage, signaling mainstream recognition of its economic significance.

signals.name: AI and Copyright Tension in Marketing
signals.description: AI-generated content raising unresolved copyright and attribution questions in marketing and media contexts, creating legal risk alongside creative opportunity.

trends.name: Culture Hacking as Brand Strategy
trends.description: Brands succeeding by embedding themselves in organic cultural moments and subcultures rather than broadcasting polished campaigns — authenticity through cultural participation.

signals.name: Gender Equity in Sports as Brand Opportunity
signals.description: Women's football and sport generating significant creative and commercial momentum for brands willing to invest ahead of mainstream recognition.

signals.name: Influencer-Brand Collaboration Evolving into Co-Creation
signals.description: Successful brand campaigns (e.g. Michael CeraVe) showing creators as genuine co-creators rather than distribution channels, fundamentally changing brand partnership models.

trends.name: Anti-AI Marketing as Differentiation
trends.description: Brands explicitly positioning against AI-generated content as a trust and authenticity signal — the first clear counter-current to AI adoption in creative work.

trends.name: Distrust and Fakery as Cultural Context
trends.description: Widespread skepticism about authenticity of media, brand communications, and public information shaping consumer receptivity to all marketing messages.

signals.name: TikTok as Primary Search for Gen Z
signals.description: Gen Z increasingly using TikTok as their primary search and discovery interface, displacing Google for product, local, and cultural queries.

signals.name: Long-Form Content Revival on YouTube
signals.description: YouTube experiencing renewed engagement with longer-form content as audiences seek depth and context in contrast to algorithmically optimized short-form.

trends.name: AI as Industry's Defining Challenge
trends.description: AI replacing previous disruptions (mobile, social) as the primary strategic question for every sector — combining opportunity and threat in unprecedented simultaneity.

signals.name: 3.5x Increase in AI Search Interest in One Year
signals.description: Google Search data showing 3.5x increase in 'artificial intelligence' searches year-over-year, marking mainstream awareness tipping point.

signals.name: Brand Purpose Under Pressure
signals.description: Purpose-driven brand positioning facing increasing skepticism and backlash as consumers distinguish authentic commitment from marketing positioning.

trends.name: M&A Strategy Shifting Under Higher Rates
trends.description: Corporate acquisition strategy fundamentally recalibrating as higher interest rate environment makes leveraged deals more expensive and strategic fit more important than financial engineering.

entries.filename: CREATOR IQ - The State of Creator Marketing 2025_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: Creator Economy Disrupting Traditional Media Buying
trends.description: Creator marketing now a primary advertising channel rather than supplement — brands allocating major budgets to creator partnerships as a core media strategy.

trends.name: Influencer Marketing Industry Scaling to $480B by 2027
trends.description: The creator economy projected to reach $480 billion in value by 2027, validating its transition from experimental to institutional marketing infrastructure.

signals.name: AI-Led Influencer Campaign Tools Maturing
signals.description: AI tools for influencer discovery, brief creation, and performance measurement becoming standard infrastructure for creator marketing programs.

signals.name: Niche Creators Outperforming Mass Influencers
signals.description: Brands reporting better ROI from highly specific niche creators versus broad-reach macro-influencers, shifting investment toward specificity over scale.

trends.name: Creator Content Outperforming Traditional Digital Advertising
trends.description: Creator-led sponsored content consistently delivering higher ROI than conventional digital advertising formats across brand and performance metrics.

signals.name: One in Four Brands Spending $1M+ on Influencers
signals.description: Significant share of brands committing seven-figure annual budgets to influencer marketing, confirming its mainstream budget category status.

signals.name: 55% of Organizations Increasing Influencer Investment YoY
signals.description: Majority of organizations growing influencer marketing allocations year-over-year, indicating structural shift rather than experimental spend.
